stemHtml: Consider a situation with high pressure at ground level, and low pressure at high level (18 000 ft or above), as for example in a cold high. How can the shape of the isobaric pressure levels be described when pictured in a vertical cross-section?

options.a: The pressure levels bulged downwards in the high-pressure area and downwards in the low-pressure area.
options.b: The pressure levels bulged upwards in the high-pressure area and upwards in the low-pressure area.
options.c: The pressure levels bulged upwards in the high-pressure area and downwards in the low-pressure area.
options.d: The pressure levels bulged downwards in the high-pressure area and upwards in the low-pressure area.
